channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: why does this keep happening — 155 replies — 346.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 155 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:17 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62270949
We'll go through this one by one

>>62269022
Pull up the btc chart on 3-1-21 it was a massive green candle day.

>>62269027
Feb 17 22 on btc you're mid bear market. Btc had large dumps before and after this announcement. Also an opinion piece is not news.

>>62269034
Jun 20 21 Your swift news broke during the middle of the mid cycle dump. Violent dumps were happening constantly in this time.

>>62269035
Apr 17 21 That was the market top. Every faggot and their mother was buying crypto dog coins and nft's.

>>62269039
Aug 24 21 In your own chart btc was already dumping prior to the talk and a speach outside of somebody important is not really news.

>>62269044
checked
Another "why does my token have a resistance level" schizo chart

62270956
It's because link is built for the BTC

62270958
BTC won. tiny linkboi peckers don't cut it.

62270979
>>62269045
Even btc was just crabbing here. It proves nothing.

>>62269049
May 10 20
see >>62268743 there were other tokens doing this same pattern but you only care about and watch link.
May 20 20
Same here
May 21 20
BTC and link are both just crabbing and dumping here. No relevance.
May 24 20
Btc goes down link goes down. No relevance.
May 29 20
Lets zoom out btc and zoom in link so it looks like that pump btc also did doesn't register as well.

>>62269051
1) idk here
2) Alts mid alt season were gaining sats, holy shit amazing take
3) Classic stop hunt. No relevance.
4.1) 4.2) This feels like you're grasping here

>>62269054
Jun 2 20
On the link chart this looks weird, but the BTC chart is literally just chopping around well before the link news.

>>62269057
Oct 10 21
>Alts did better than btc mid alt season
Even still BTC was a doji candle this day and ranup heavily after

>>62269059
Another "why resistance on my token chart"

62270997
>>62270949
>>62270979
It does not matter if Bitcoin is in a bear, a crab, or a bull; the second Link does something Bitcoin nosedives.
Your efforts here only prove that.

Literally how are you this retarded.

62271020
>>62269063
Oct 21 21
>Why did btc just drop during the peak of the wack off distribution pattern which was also mid alt season, don't they know alts were all pumping

>>62269065
Nov 15 21
You mean the pre-anticipatory run-up for the conference was sold off after the conference........WHAT???????

>>62269070
Apr 17 21
We discussed this day already.
Sep 13 21
Cherry pick

>>62269132
>Bitcoin crashed the entire market
As it does, and it will continue until the market actually matures. And even after certain cryptos will be lumped together into industries. Why does Apple's stock go down when bad news happens for Microsoft? Because they are both tech stocks, and people's minds think that what is bad for one must be bad for the other, even if they technically aren't.

>>62269280
>nobody gives a shit about twitter posts
And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters. Everyone just sits in their own comfy place with only the ideas they like. 4 chan is meant to be that open place but it's fill with chuds and incels ( like yourselves) that repeat talking points en masse and don't actually listen to differing input.

62271032
>future of finance
Mais non, mon ami
Is ze future of France, honhonhon
Ker-BOOOOOOOOOMBE
HONHONHONHON

62271040
>>62269410
I think it happens because anons want the idea of "they are out to get you" to be true, and you look for anything to rationalize this to convince yourselves.

>>62269433
>Why they are doing this?
Because so much about random 4chan posters so they devote all their time to create crazy market dumps the exact millisecond anytime they feel joy about their investments which is why your life suck because they have it out for you and not because you spend all day jerking off and posting 4chan....This actually does happen to some degree but it's algorithms farming you

>>62269448
>the people with enough money to suppress entire markets and start/stop wars among countries need more money to gain the power they already have
yes of course

>>62269628
What does making it look like for you?

>>62269680
Sadly probably allot lower than we all want it to be depending is cll releases their financials and implements staking fully and even then it won't be the singularity anons are after.

>>62269681
>they are constantly monitoring /biz/
not the tranime posters cry face

>>62269710
Cll has actively hinted at a not well divulged hiding strategy, which, ergo, would reveal the strategy, and nobody knows what they do with the token or the company in its entirety.

62271062
>>62270997
Yeah duuuuuude it makes perfect sense, whether we are in a bull, bear, or crab market BTC just seems to always giga dump on either link news or pumps, but this is totally normal and coincidental bro duuuuuuuude.
DUUUUUUUUUDE it's not like BTC dumps on LINK news hehe it's just that BTC randomly dumps and takes LINK down with it hehe take your meds dude

62271066
>>62269780
>The unwashed masses who will ask
How many of them have an iphone or android but don't buy apple stock or microsoft? Now tell people to invest into the companies that make software for those devices. It's a moot point to think they're fudding the masses from buying the services that prediction markets or draft kings will use. Link won't matter to these people.

>>62269789
this

>>62269848
The light and dark both exist inside you. There is no inherently evil or good on this earth. Get 10 layers into this kind of thinking and you'll start to see how great God really is to be aware and manage all this.

>>62270997
Link anons have a persecution bias, and won't show you the times their "theories" don't apply.

For example all the charts I responded too are pretty close in the date range. Being all last cycle. Why none from the 2017 era? Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range or even the most recent link news? It's from one anon making the chart and the echo chamber sharing it back and forth for years. It wasn't true to begin with, you've just seen it so many times and look at the backlash I'm getting for even saying I disagree with their narrative. You're not arguing with logic points but the anons own ego.

62271074
>>62271066
>Link anons have a persecution bias
That's pretty normal after like 6 years of outright persecution.

>Why none from the 2017 era?
Here's one from 2018

62271088
>>62271066
>It's a moot point to think they're fudding the masses from buying the services that prediction markets or draft kings will use.
Why do you even answer to me if you don't even understand what I'm saying?
I never said anything about 'fudding' the masses (that's a freudian slip on your part).
In fact they do the opposite, they openly spoonfeed with all the marketing that CLL and Swift are doing so that anyone can buy in now while they suppress and distribute the tokens.
You are so fucking new here buddy and it shows. If you were here since day 1 you would understand that Chainlink was always supposed to be le fair crypto-native network that banks just seemed to adopt because le pristine track record and so on and so forth.
This is of course complete BS because Chainlink IS a Swift project so it's king made, but it could not seem like that for DeFi adoption to look decentralized and for their future plans which you don't seem to grasp at all.
Don't worry, I know that you are just the verbose ripple shill so you have a very sallow understanding of Chainlink..
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHA TAKE A LOAD OF THIS GUY

62271096
>>62271088
>>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
The ENTIRE 2022-2023 (((range))) was ENTIRELY due to BTC dump-based LINK suppression keeping the price in that range.

62271099
>>62271074
Not the gotcha you believe this is. Another lesson from trading stocks. The same people tend to trade and hold the same stocks over and over. What this means is every chart has a temperament to it. Like how there are cats, but not all cats are the same. Got it? Different temperament. So the link chart, from what you all continually show, is that links temperament is for consistent flat resistance levels. This probably won't change for the link until enough new investors/traders join the morphus blob of temperament to change it. Which is good because it lends some credibility to link repeating the same patterns so you can profit from them.

62271104
>>62271099
>Why none from the 2017 era?
>Gets shown one from the 2017 era
>Instant wall of pilpul
hahahaha

62271106
>>62271096
lel I was just about to post this.
This faggot is as blind and clueless as he is wordy and spammy.

And now he's going all new age on us:
>>62271099
>What this means is every chart has a temperament to it.

Dude's about to break out the mood crystals.

62271116
>>62271020
>And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters
you clearly do, which is why you are here calling people "schizos" for noticing BTC dumps whenever LINK gets positive news (pic rel). i dont spend a single minute discussing people in xrp threads or twitter: why would you post 26 times in this thread?

you have done this before havent you

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

Please shut the fuck up. You are gay. Muuh spirituals.

62277201
>>62273954
You're theories only make sense to 4chan posters with a persecution kink. Bring them outside this site and you're into heavy schizo territory.

62277225
>>62277201
Fallacy ad populum, faggot. You argue like a woman

62277275
Its weird checking in on this board like once a week and seeing the usual paid fud just never stops, and how much of it just never gets responded to or probably even seen
There's at least like 10-20k hours worth of collective fuddie manpower that would have made no difference if it had never existed at all. It would be tragic if the time of third world subhumans was worth anything to begin with

62277295
>>62277225
>the stuff we say is true because we can bias it into reality y...you're just wrong for disagreeing with our spam
Half this thread is top of the debate pyramid calling me retarded every other post. You faggots wonder why ironic shit posting is all that happens here.

item.title: /BIZ/ YLYL — 61 replies — 244.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BIZ/ YLYL

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:37 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272176
>>62272167
lost again

62273212
>>62271586
>Thank you Walmart economic goyslave unit #347658

62273259
>>62270199
I'm so Fucking Happy I left office Jobs behind in 2022

62273308
>>62272126
me except with my SOXL options

62273842
I seen this on group chat gave me hardy kek

62273847
>>62273842
Possibly the least funny thing I’ve ever seen. Off yourself.

62274126
>>62273847
Being a peak faggot still pretty popular in these part

62274144
>>62271628
Only white teenage girls who work at American fast food drive-in joints should be doing the whole rollerblade server thing.

62274580
>>62273179
losted again

62274585
>>62274242
more kek if brown hand holding euro notes for curry

62274618
>>62272398
Finally some good new content

62276307
Job Posting: Goy Cattle (Entry-Level Herd)
Company: Shekelberg Global Asset ManagementLocation: The Real World™ (No Remote)
Salary: $17k + participation trophies
Type: Lifelong Indenture

About Us
We manage the money. You manage the reality. Perfect partnership.

Position Summary
Hiring motivated Goy Cattle to handle all the physical, taxable, everyday bullshit while we short futures from the penthouse.

Key Responsibilities
• Live in the Real World™ daily: traffic, kneeling, $12 eggs, and pointless cannon fodder.

• Consume goyslop, pay taxes, and reproduce below replacement rate.
• Rage on social media about culture wars while we tweak interest rates.
• Vote blue/red (both approved), stay outraged on schedule, then return to Nigflix for goyslop and real world programming.
• Die quietly after 40 years without noticing patterns.

Qualifications
• High school diploma, (college debt a plus).
• Zero pattern recognition and foreskin.
• Loves onions, forever wars, and believing the news.
• Must identify as goy. Self-ID accepted.
What We Offer
• Inflation that always wins.
• Health insurance tax (claimable if brown and jobless only).
• 1vacation days + daily slavery and holocaust infotainment .
• Premium distractions: sportsball, OnlyFans, and election theater.

Equal Opportunity
We hire all goyim — White, Black, Brown, etc. The more divided, the easier to manage.

How to ApplyEmail résumé + soul to goyim@shekelberg.globalSubject: “Ready to Graze.”

62277367
>>62276307
Lost hard

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 51 replies — 204.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:29 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62276513
>>62276484
>What interoperability solution are they testing?
None. It's not about interoperability as far as I can tell.
>Chainlink will be integrated into DTCC’s Collateral AppChain to enable the seamless pairing of asset prices, valuations and movement with the aim of overhauling how market risk is managed globally. CRE is built to operate at institutional scale and provides access to a resilient data and orchestration layer, unlocking automated workflows for the Collateral AppChain’s advanced eligibility, valuation, margining, collateral optimization, settlement, and related post-trade processes. Rather than relying on one-off integrations, CRE provides a reusable framework that enables DTCC’s Collateral AppChain to scale across new data types, asset classes and collateral use cases.
>“By leveraging tokenization and distributed ledger technology (DLT) to modernize collateral mobility, our goal is to enable 24/7, near real-time collateral management across global markets and blockchains,” said Nadine Chakar, DTCC Managing Director, Global Head of Digital Assets. “The integration of Chainlink’s CRE and data standard will allow us to deliver a unified on-chain environment, bringing on-chain asset prices, valuations and other collateral agreement data to support this transformative industry initiative.”
https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

62276529
>>62276476
>It can’t function on other chains.
Read nigga read:
>The CLPR protocol is designed to be chain-agnostic and support major EVM-compatible networks, including Ethereum, Hyperledger Besu and Avalanche subnets. Any network that can produce or verify state proofs is a candidate for CLPR, regardless of whether it is a public or private network.
https://hashgraph.com/clpr/

62276532
>>62276355
>infinite liquidity, bridgeless bridging, oracle workflows
the same shit is spouted by the marketing department of every oracle and bridging solution that comes out. in the end they all work the same way. you read the state from somewhere offchain and you relay it on-chain. it is not rocket science and there is no magic bullet.

62276544
>>62276513
>None. It's not about interoperability as far as I can tell.

>DTCC Champions “Interoperability” as the Key to Industry Adoption of Digital Asset Securities

https://www.dtcc.com/dtcc-connection/articles/2026/march/04/building-interoperable-digital-asset-markets

Just kill yourself.

62276551
>>62276476
enough w the gaslighting, no one knows shit except hbag and CLL.

62276554
>>62276098
Word
Sword
Swords

62276559
>>62276544
You are confused. That's from March 2026, not May 2026, so different things. Your link is just a generic press release that has nothing to do with what is going in production later this year. The DTTC thing that is going live with Chainlink is CRE for collateral management.

62276570
>>62276559
>You are confused. That's from March 2026, not May 2026, so different things
No.

>The DTTC thing that is going live with Chainlink is CRE for collateral management.
Which includes CCIP. https://chain.link/hyperledger-besu-interoperability

62276577
>>62276551
hedera is late to the party, state proofs have been floated as a bridging solution for years and they just aren't practical whatsoever.

62276580
>>62276570
Yes NIGGER I already responded to that argument here >>62276103
CCIP is part of CRE but they are not using CCIP only CRE.

62276587
>>62276577
>state proofs have been floated as a bridging solution for years and they just aren't practical whatsoever.
Can you expand on that? I'm actually interested in the tech unlike the other niggers ITT.

62276590
>>62276580
>>62276513
>https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

>The Chainlink stack provides the essential data, INTEROPERABILTY, compliance, and privacy standards needed to power advanced blockchain use cases for institutional tokenized assets, lending, payments, stablecoins, and more.

>CCIP is part of CRE but they are not using CCIP only CRE.
Theyre using every product chainlink offers.

62276608
>>62276590
>>The Chainlink stack provides the essential data, INTEROPERABILTY, compliance, and privacy standards needed to power advanced blockchain use cases for institutional tokenized assets, lending, payments, stablecoins, and more.
You took that from the generic "About Chainlink" paragraph at the bottom of the article, which has NOTHING to do with the press release in and of itself, which ends at the "###" symbol.

62276626
>>62276608
It has everything to do with the press release because its mentioned in the press release lol

So if the DTCCs states ""Interoperability” as the Key to Industry Adoption" which solution are they using? Seems like its the most important part of this all working. How does Besu communicate with other chains?

62276643
>>62276626
>Seems like its the most important part of this all working
Most important? Look nigger they seem to only be using Besu and Canton so obviously the interoperability thing is not that big of a priority considering they don't give two shits about public DLTs.

62276662
>>62276643
>Most important?
https://www.dtcc.com/dtcc-connection/articles/2026/march/04/building-interoperable-digital-asset-markets

>DTCC Champions “Interoperability” as the Key to Industry Adoption of Digital Asset Securities

Are you retarded?

62276697
>>62276662
There you are again talking about a different press release.
We are talking about the May 2026 press release, not the March 2026 one.
Are you a bot or what?

62276699
>>62276587
blockchains are typically merkle trees that produce a new "state root" with every block. this is what keeps a blockchain verifiable all the way back through genesis. a "state proof" proves that a block was legitimately produced, and since transaction hashes in the block are used to generate the block hash, you can prove that a given transaction occurred in that block. This is how blockchains have always worked and we used "state proofs" all the time to know what the true state of the chain is. Verifying a state proof is easy to do off-chain, because we have access to the whole state and can check the proof against that state, but in the case of a bridging solution, a recipient blockchain has no knowledge of the state of sender blockchains, and therefore needs to be somehow told what the state is so it can check the proof. It's a tantalizing idea because state proofs are such a fundamental concept to blockchains, that surely one blockchain could prove claims about its state to another blockchain, but because they are essentially blind to one another, it can't happen unless the validators of the recipient blockchain literally communicate with the validators of the sending blockchain to verify the state. interconnecting the networks that way. otherwise you need to rely on a third party to do that work for you, and trust that their signature of the data is valid instead. here, for example, is fucking algorand from 2022 talking about their state proof bridging and oracle solution:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LEsaen_kMUU

62276716
>>62276697
Fuck off you stupid inbred nigger.

62276743
>>62276699
Checked and thank you, finally something interesting. So the Hedera guys are calling this "bridgless" because the validators of DLT A communicate (via state proofs) with the validators of DLT B without a third party (like Chainlink CCIP). I didn't know about Algorand trying to do something like this before. So why do you think this kind of think isn't practical? Idk about the tech at all I'm kinda retarded in that regard so idk if CLPR could scale or not.
Some HBAR baggies at the HBAR sub are claiming stuff like
>can be executed unilaterally; industry cooperation is not needed. Mance around 5min says and reiterates, industry participation would be nice, but will be implemented with or without them. unilaterally. massive.

62276820
>>62276743
it can be "executed unilaterally" in the same way that any bridging solution can be deployed without permission. without industry cooperation, they will need smart contract endpoints like any other bridge, and that contract will be responsible for validating any incoming messages. this means a permissioned bridge validator is responsible for forwarding the message (just like every other bridge) and it will sign the message using a commitment on hedera's ledger, so you (the user) can verify on hedera that indeed the message has a valid state proof associated with it, but the part that matters (the smart contract) can't see hedera's ledger, so it's hedera's signature that the contract trusts.

62276868
>>62276699
>>62276820
>more gaslighting
idk why the only strat here is just making shit up. Youre simply reciting the status quo; meanwhile the guy who invented this, who also invented the hashgraph, is calling it as big or bigger than the development of the L1. This a retarded approach. CLL and Hbar are partners and there is a far greater likelihood theyre working together than it is being stabbed in the back.

62276882
>>62276743
while were both on the same team, this guy’s coping strategy is just literally making shit up. no one knows how it works the details arent published. not only that, the founder is calling it a marvel. i give more creedence to a name who is running his own l1, than some random 4channer.

I don’t see CLPR as a threat in the slightest. However it would be really cool to hear from CLL about this.

62276896
>>62276868
>>62276882
lemonhead did not invent the merkle tree, that was ralph merkle. he also did not invent the idea of a state proof bridge.
>recite the status quo
I am educating you, the credulous rube, about how these things fundamentally work. Be grateful dear

62276899
>>62276882
>the founder is calling it a marvel
lol obviously you can't trust that to mean anything because all founders will suck their own cock so that you buy their coin/token, I mean they talked about the hashgraph a lot too as if it would replace every other DLT and at the end of the day no one is currently using Hedera for anything whatsoever.
>I don’t see CLPR as a threat in the slightest. However it would be really cool to hear from CLL about this.
This I see as wishful thinking because according to you there are zero details on how CLPR works so how can you not see it as a threat when they literally use the words "oracle workflows" in their marketing.

62276940
>>62276896
>strawman and gaslighting
whatever dude continue alienating us. what youre saying is the exact opposite an L1 founder worth a billion dollars and his name/face/reputation is in the public sphere, you are simply. this is retarded. you dont know, I dont know, only big serg and hbag actually know. The reality is, people are going to be watching his CLPR video, and will 100% misinterpret what theyre saying as some nightmare scenario for CLL like the faggots on this board are doing right now.
>>62276899
yeah I agree with your first point, I hope thats true. as far as the second point, CLL is part of the council they probably helped write the release. Chainlink literally governs Hbar. Thats why.

62276984
>>62276940
>CLL is part of the council they probably helped write the release
There's no way. Just no it doesn't makes sense. Why would CLL spend their resources on making something that replaces CCIP. Also in the Hedera videos they use quite aggresive language obviously positioning themselves against CCIP even if they don't say it outloud.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F5uEYJL1ups

62277016
>>62276984
whatever, we all will speculate bc no one actually knows, but it is a fact that CLL literally governs hbag. would be really great to hear from CLL.

62277251
>>62275877
>>62275893
>fudcuck can't even wait 5 mins to hop ids and bump his CURRENT THING thread
Pantone 448 C subhumans have only one trick and they've wasted thousands of hours on here achieving nothing
Herbs

62277340
Why do they do this? They say CLPR doesn't rely on an intermediary and it's "direct ledger-to-ledger", but CLPR still consists of off-chain routing severs to read, pass and write data between chains. It's still a wrapped token or a burn-and-mint. Who runs the CLPR nodes and how is their honest behavior guaranteed?

item.title: SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. — 27 replies — 108.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP.

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:32 UTC

OP

SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. Why aren't you investing in their IPO?

Early replies (first 3)

62273501
>>62273487
If AI ends up being a nothing burger, then SpaceX is little more than a flashy telecommunications company.
Everyone in the world is betting the farm on AI too, what happens if a different company achieves AGI before SpaceX?

62273502
People still don't understand what printing money does to an economy.

62273506
>>62273487
>SpaceX
>Space
the name of the fucking company is 0.1% of its TAM golly gee maybe they should call it AIX?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273511
>>62273487
Space is bigger than America so the profits are infinite
Elon really knows how to win

62273537
I'll probably give em a hundred bucks, see how it goes.

62273604
Everyone holding a broad index fund, which is pretty much every American with a retirement account, will be inadvertently investing in this IPO. We will all be Elong Muk's exit liquidity. I can't wait to support a company that loses $5 billion/year.

62273611
>>62273604
Can't you just sell your index fund and then buy it later once the stock has dropped 90%?

62273615
IDK about space, but I'm bullish on tesla and U.S. stocks. I still think rockets are a scam and they already have UFO's that are capable of long distance space travel.

By 2030 solid state battery electric cars will be here in full force in tesla for consumers, making ev's and they'll be exceptional deals, 300K miles with barely any wear compared to a gas engine car. Incredibly efficient compared to gas cars and last longer.

Agentic AI will be in cars, self driving will be good by then.

62273662
>>62273487
"SpaceX estimates it's own TAM by making up a bunch of bullshit"

62273749
>>62273511
Its not, according to muricans.

62274456
>>62273749
Well, it’s not any bigger than Texas.
That’s why they have to launch the rockets from Texas.

62274498
>>62273487
SpaceX TAM eternally va US GDP In one year.

62274836
>>62274498
TAM is measured in annual revenue

62275036
>>62273487
Maybe if he was mining asteroids for precious metals, but this is ridiculous
Twitter is not worth that much money
Grok isn't worth that much money
This is a scam

62275067
>>62275036
Asteroid mining is the kind of thing that sounds like a good idea, until you think about the practicalities of running mining equipment and refining plants in microgravity, the delta-v requirements of bringing the stuff home, and the likely lack of any easily accessible ices that could be turned into rocket propellant on any asteroid that orbits anywhere near the sun

62275088
>Why aren't you investing in their IPO?
There aren't enough shares for plebs like me to short.
Same reason I sat in the cuck chair as people in my discord made six figures on CBRS shorts. No volume for plebbies.

62275111
I am going to put $500 into SpaceX and then hopefully one day long after I am dead my money will make it to the moons of Saturn

62275148
>>62275111
It will moke likely crash into Uranus after the IPO

62275195
>>62273487
>IPO
Point me to an IPO that wasn't a bag-holder hand-off from private equity and didn't immediately lose large chunks of its value after the first day.

62275335
>>62273611
The problem isn't losing money; it's tens of millions if not more basically being forced to have a stake in what's going to be a shit show of an IPO. Most people won't even know that this is happening.

There are also a lot of people with target date retirement funds and reallocating their portfolio, while doable, isn't something that they should have to worry about because Nasdaq changed the rules to allow mega-caps to enter the Nasdaq-100 after 15 days following the IPO. The same thing is going to happen with Scam Altman-Bankman-Fried's OpenAI when that goes public.

62276783
>>62273615
By definition these would not be UFOs then since UFO stands for unidentified flight object.

62276797
>>62273487
It's a scam. I read it's IPO. Unaudited financials. Absolute disaster. There are dog coins with better fundementals

62276808
>>62275335
Pension funds won't even touch software at the moment let alone this shitshow. reddit has this deep need to give Elon their money no matter what but as a stock it is hot garbage.

62276827
>>62275195
Mixue (2097.HK) had a three-month long +100% bull run just after its IPO last year, but now it’s down of course

I don’t think Google/Alphabet ever went back to its IPO level once

62277088
>>62276797
>Unaudited financials.
[citation needed]

62277349
>>62276808
Nigga Reddit hates musky

62277355
>>62275195
Facebook

item.title: On the Psychology of Long-term Bagholders — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
On the Psychology of Long-term Bagholders

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:42 UTC

OP

Any good reads on the (patho)psychology of long term bag holders? GME, BBY, LINK, Bayhorse, any other of the many, many shitcoins. I find these groups very fun and interesting to observe, and I'm wondering if there have been any good scholarly works on these populations. What makes them tick? What blinds them so? Why must the hold? How can I ensure I avoid such a fate? How can I help friends of similar delusions?

Early replies (first 3)

62275443
>>62275427
It’s the sunk cost fallacy played out. Some people would rather become “part of the community” instead of taking the L and moving on.

62275453
>>62275443
That's a good point re community. I'd imagine many don't have other communities to be a part of and/or check them, so there is value to being part of this group, even if it costs you financially.

62275567
>>62275427
So you mean like normal investing. Buying a stock and being long on it. Do you also sneed about people who stacked silver for years like a retard? sage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275610
>>62275427
Hope bias, sunken cost fallacy, they learned to disregard any information not from their bubble as fud, self supported more and more unrealistic narratives in their bubble.

62277126
>>62275427
im one. i recognize I'm retarded, I also think I understand the value proposition of chain link. Being retarded, yet "understanding the value proposition" has trapped me because I know with %100 certainty I'm too retarded to make it any other way than holding link

62277147
>>62275427
Link actually does something, none of those other things do anything. Despite having the largest integrations and usecase in the entirety of crypto for some reason Links price doesn't move though. Everything I've just said is also 100% undeniable fact. The only difference between people acknowledging Link is the price moving or not. It moves and suddely every integration is real and investors were genius all along.

62277276
>>62275567
CHAINLINK =\= legit investment

62277336
>>62275427
Don't forget xrp

62277352
>>62275567
Are we really comparing de nice Chainlink token which is used for nothing to silver?

62277389
>>62275427
I hold a green position in one of these. Why should I sell or justify it to some 0dte indiafag?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:36 UTC

OP

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

Early replies (first 3)

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62275723

62276473
>>62275591

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62276491
Why would you ever trust a Jew to do anything but steal from you…?

62276498
Its still above LINK in mcap

62276961
>>62275591
More evidence of Cardanus imploding?

62277005
>I devoted years of my life to a ghostchain that does literally nothing

Skill issue

62277151
>>62275591
Charles' whole empire is imploding

https://protos.com/charles-hoskinsons-250m-clinic-to-close-after-buying-up-nfts-and-robots/

62277363
>>62277151
Noooooo not the tranny support center!!!!1!1!1

62277373
>>62275591
It sucks to see a well-known coin go through such a bad situation, but that's why focusing on truly decentralized coins is the most important. Those that can't be mined in pools or via GPU/ASIC/FPGA, like Nerva/XNV, ensure everything is actually decentralized and split across the entire ecosystem.

item.title: What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day? — 44 replies — 33.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-05-26 21:25 UTC

OP

What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Early replies (first 3)

62274691
>>62274690
Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.

62274797
>>62274691
>zero iq take
Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass, which means theyre probably the ones going to implement it.

62274806
>>62274797
>Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass
Irrelevant. CLPR makes CCIP obsolete.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275140
>>62274690
KABOOOOOOOOOOM

62275167
>>62274963
Fucking when
I’d be ecstatic to have $0.01 per LINK per day at this point

62275273
>>62275104

Low 7 figs per year is not some magical unattainable # in the future American economy.

System is rigged bias is the dumbest low IQ take in history.

System is incompetent, slow and inefficient. But eventually gets to the stable value.

62275301
>>62274797
>Theyre on the Hedera council

anyone else find it hilarious all these grand titles crypto scammers give themselves

one week they're poker players, failed salesmen, etc., then they join a crypto company as an executive board member. then their gullible investors give themselves battle names (army, rangers, marines, etc)

reminds me of the larpers from role models

62275343
>>62275167
that's not realistic, HOWEVER you will have LINK at $0.01 eventually.

62275385
>>62274986
>>62274841
>>62274806
>>62274691
There's a reason CLPR is only live on Hedera itself; it's impossible to implement on other chains without either incurring massive gas costs, or compromising on data security by gimping the system.

CLPR works like Cosmos or Avalanche in that there's a registry of validators. Keeping this registry alive on a foreign chain requires continuous state tracking. This means massive amounts of gas.
Bypassing this gas cost involves cutting corners. Massive ones.

62275388
>>62274690
those banners go for about $300 at fedex office.

62275410
>>62274690
>>62274848
how would an asset worth $9 pay out $1 per day? where does the money come from?

62275479
>>62275410
you are talking to iq80 retards who also yolo'd their meager savings and have no psychological way out besides suicide or delusions.

62275500
>>62275410
Staking returns aren't proportional to the Link price, they're proportional to usage fees which are measured in USD.

62275555
>>62274690
Sounds great when does this start fellow marine?

62275595
>>62274690
>Stinkies really thought some stinky russian made a magical piggie bank that would give them returns beyond their wildest dreams

62275635
>>62275555
Two weeks

Impressive digits btw

62275645
>>62274690
chainlink staking is economic security and the incentive is to pay $1 per link per day? good! KABOOM

62275660
>>62274690
What do you not understand about crypto being dead, Web3 never happening, and Chainlink’s “use case” being obsolete?

62275685
no I meant the price of chainlink will be equivalent to $1for every day of the week
I'm not talking about rewards or anything else
the price of chainlink will be $7

62275738
>>62275660
... and then you woke up and realized DTCC is going into production with Chainlink later this year.

62275779
>>62275385
you said that in another thread, whered you get this info? post sauce

62275789
>>62275738
Oh wow, two years of insiders trading at low volume before people realize tokenized RWA are stupid because why even do that when there’s no chance of outsized returns and doing fraud which were unironically crypto’s biggest selling points?

62275818
>>62275779
>you said that in another thread
I definitely did not, I only researched this myself today.

This is all public information, Hedera, Cosmos, Avalanche have it all laid out in their documentation.

>>62275789
just look at this incoherent seethebabble

62275885
>>62275385
>it's impossible to implement on other chains
This is not what they are saying, just check the hedera sub, it's looking rather grim for ccip

62275919
>>62275818
So no actual counterargument? All you have to do is show me any evidence that DTCC tokenized assets are generating ANY hype right now.

62275933
>>62274690
Ok. But.... price?? Oh.. and chart???

62275992
>>62275933
going up

62276072
>>62275789
lolwut

62276114
>>62275500
>usage fees
What usage?

62276153
>>62274691
>Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.
/thread

62276166
>>62275885
>This is not what they are saying
It's not what I'm saying either, you just suck at not being dumb.

>>62276114
DTCC later this year, for instance.

62276216
I'd say chainlink has sizeable state in HBAR. Sergey is not dumb he knew the writing was on he wall when no oher DLT could be as fast or cheap as HBAR.

62277103
>>62275479
ive been found out

item.title: Fuck my life — 7 replies — 17.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Fuck my life

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:20 UTC

OP

>My job is officially requiring us to come to the office 3 times a week
GG.

Early replies (first 3)

62277146
my father worked for 10 years from home. he is going to retire in a few months, thats around the time when they changed it from perma wfh to 4days a week minimum at the office. its pretty much over for wfh guys. bosch btw, I guess other big companies are following suit.

62277153
my father worked for 10 years from home. he is going to retire in a few months, thats around the time when they changed it from perma wfh to 4days a week minimum at the office. its pretty much over for wfh guys. bosch btw, I guess other big companies are following suit.

62277177
my father worked for 10 years from home. he is going to retire in a few months, thats around the time when they changed it from perma wfh to 4days a week minimum at the office. its pretty much over for wfh guys. bosch btw, I guess other big companies are following suit.

62277294
my father worked for 10 years from home. he is going to retire in a few months, thats around the time when they changed it from perma wfh to 4days a week minimum at the office. its pretty much over for wfh guys. bosch btw, I guess other big companies are following suit.

62277304

62277309
>>62277146
Boomers are such lucky faggots it's unreal.
They're retiring just as things are going tits up.

item.title: NOT A DRILL — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NOT A DRILL

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-26 22:32 UTC

OP

YouTube is demonetizing automated channels left and right. Youtube automation was supposed to be a stable source of passive income and now people are losing their empires. Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?

Early replies (first 3)

62277230
>>62277227
Cry me a river, dickface.

62277233
>>62277227
Saar...

62277354
>>62277227
>Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?
They needed people to upload AI slop for that data to be fed into the algorithms to train them to punish that content.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

>>62277297

item.title: /Biz revival — 125 replies — 83.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 125 • Updated: 2026-05-26 21:14 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271298
>>62271126
because it's like buying litecoin with a discount, and when ltc pumps LITS will pump multiple X more just like micro strategy did with btc

62271371
>>62265467
>Ton
Turns out that's in my bookmarks as something with potential. I looked at Litecoin when I first got into crypto, but I figured these long-standing projects like it, LINK, HBAR, etc. were things I'd come back to later with gains from lower market cap stuff. Seems that was the correct decision since so many of them are now around the same price as they day I bought my first assets.
I figured they might make great strides someday but not in a hurry since they'd already make great strides (price-wise) since their debuts. Despite that I'm shocked to see Litecoin is like half the price of when I first looked at it.
I'd recommend some stuff myself but I'm still in that newish area where I've made good picks after reading up on stuff before buying (the DYOR part /biz/ always told me to do) but couldn't briefly articulate why someone should take a look at something at current prices. Everything that's done me well is more expensive now than when I first bought it.

62271377
>>62265877
I don't think there's a LUNC thread up now but I remember seeing one recently where there was a lot of reasonable optimism.

62271378
>>62265467
It's dead Jim

62271387
>>62270867
What's the basis for uranium at this time? I saw a few months ago people talking about various reactors, from new mini ones to restarting some existing ones.

62271407
>>62271387
an anon shilled some us stock, nano nuclear energy and i didn't buy because the chart looked already pumped. it went down a lot since like 40%

Nano nuclear energy. I looked it up they're trying to develop mini nuclear reactors thant can be moved around, like for data centers etc. But i'm wondering isn't that tech super risky?in any case that might or might not be a great moonshot. No idea, don't own any but looking at it

62271421
>>62271407
I can see what you would be. I remember seeing some airplane-lifted reactors some months ago. Operation Windlord. Valar Atomics, though, they are not publicly traded.
I see what you mean about NNE having doing about a 10x since it began and now being like 50% down since then. Still, nice to have specific leads on what to research.

62271501
>>62265467
Its dead because there havent been enough nigger days due to the bull market

62271653
How about starting with removing this faggot nigger captcha and having to wait for a minute for it? I gave up posting here, because of it, honestly. And I'm not verifying my email each time either.

62271675
>>62271653
Captchas become faster and easier if you post more

62271760
>>62271675
and then reset your progress for no reason
no, I didn't erase my cookies, just couldn't post for TWO DAYS

62271767
>>62271760
yes jannies trannies destroyed it all. it's over. At least biz is the only white board left

62271790
>>62265467
good pick with litecoin anon, literally the only coin which can reliably process frequent micropayments without clogging or taking too many fees


it will be the coin of choice for autonomous AI agents to transact with eachother in the future

62271899
>>62265467
what killed this place to begin with is that there is no business or finance talk going on here. Its just retarded "buy this crypto coin!!!"

62272124
>>62265467
>LTC
Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.

>TON
Shit is never going to pump.

>UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.
>IF
They won't ever back down their bullshit, even in the deepest shit they still prefered to appeal to niggers and fags instead of making games.

62272342
>>62272124
>Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.
they just developped MWEB transactions anon. Try harder.

why wouldn't ton pump? are you retarded?

nvgmi

62272366
>>62265467
india and china need to be banned from this board

62273281
>>62271298
Seems like it crashes X times more too unfortunately. Maybe a good idea for ltc exposure but I’m really unsure if litecoin will be the play next bull

62273290
>>62265467
>we need to revive biz
>how do we do it you ask
>well its simple you just buy my decade old bags of a shitcoin so i can finally break even
well guess we found aid devastated bald whiny fag and its op

62274410
>>62269707
Yeah I think Litecoin "trojan horses" privacy into mainstream cryptocurrency. The marketing around it has always been "OG coin".
Eventually the markets will realize privacy is useful for more than cipherpunkery, and is embraced by big business.
Related, there was a lot of ZK research done by Polygon teams, but their funding ran out, I think? There's some other smaller projects experimenting with very compact proofs, Mina Protocol I think. I haven't researched that much. I'm a fan of LTC for being OG - strong codebase.

62274471
>>62273281
crashes x times more

not really. right now the price of the stock is like a 20% discount over their litecoin treasury. it gets giga hyped if litecoin moons. Now if litecoin were to moon for real one day (let's say 800$) this stock would probably x 50 from where it is.
The thing i don't like about LITS is basically not your key, not your cryptos. So i'm 30% LITS 70% litecoin

>>62274410
it's really a revolution. I think btc will implement the same thing. It's an integrated coin mixer that can't be traced basically.

62275749
>>62272342
few people know that aside from the privacy aspect, mweb also makes the network scalable because of pruning

without pruning a blockchain ends up taking infinite storage space

62276184
>>62274410
>>62274471
This leading to btc implementing something similar would be insane. XMR will likely always be the king but some more mainstream privacy adoption will be a net positive I think

62276504
>>62275749
didn't know that. I guess so far blockchain size wasn't really an issue because it's not so big and you can run pruned nodes already? maybe i was mistaken.

>>62276184
now that i think about it, i'm 100% positive this is going to happen. Privacy is a huge issue for crypto, and a huge positive when it is implemented. Too many home jacking, fraud, taxes, you avoid all this shit. Bitcoin coin mixers were a weak option to try to anonymize btc. Mweb basically wash any coin in and out and will be as hard to trace as monero. Hopefully litecoin gets to shine because of it before btc implements it.

62276517
>>62276504

62276943
>>62276517
BALDY BALDY OVER THERE,
WHAT’S IT LIKE TO HAVE NO HAIR?
IS IT HOT OR IS IT COLD?
I DON’T KNOW ‘CAUSE I'M NOT BALD!

62276952
>>62276943
Knew it was you

62276981
>>62276952
the id change itself for no reason sometimes idk why

62276989
>>62276981
Sure sure....

62277056
>>62265475
>Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
Scam Altman

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 83 replies — 37.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-05-26 20:30 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273638
>>62273575
Garbage? I think you don't have money to buy btc at this level. Poor

62273643
>>62273547
memecoins

62273653
>>62273638
Oh but I do.
I'm just basically not going to buy any.
Sorry, not being your exit liquidity again. Have done that enough over the years.

62273655
no value, no real use. i dont believe in it. i dont care if it goes to 1million

62273660
>>62271712
>What’s stopping you from buying BTC?
I don't like people who own it and sell it. I don't want to give them my real money. I think it would be good if everyone stopped giving them money. They don't deserve the lavish lives that they are living by selling bitcoin to people like you.

62273787
>>62273635
You should probably look into Babylon first before calling everything centralized.
A lot of people still don’t understand the native staking and self-custody side of BTCFi yet.

62273791
>>62271965
Are you into core?

62273794
>>62272143
Like Gold and sliver? kek

62273812
>>62272874
>moving huge wealth out of a country
Only brown people leave their home country. Only criminals do tax evasion. I'm neither of those.

62273814
>>62271712
lack of desire to throw my money away to a kike scam

62273819
>>62271935
Ok but a lot depends on your risk tolerance and what kind of returns you’re expecting from this stage of the market.

62273824
>>62272659
>too bad you didnt buy at $200 or you'd be retired
retired on what? US dollars from selling your shitty worthless digital ponzi coin? so you admit bitcoin has no purpose or use other than hoping other retards buy it to pump up the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273959
>>62273824
So you're buying stocks in the hope some retards will pump the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273985
>>62271712
The narrative keeps shifting. First it was supposed to finally defeat the dollar, then it was supposed to be ATMs everywhere, then it's supposed to be digital gold, then the government and blackrock buy it, then it's not SUPPOSED to be in atms and then it's just really a leverage tech stock without any tech.

It's insanity. How am I supposed to know what the price will be in 10 years if the narrative cycle is like in 2?

62273994
>>62273787
This guy who spams this in every discussion has convinced me that what he's talking about is a scam and I'll never use it.

62274011
>>62271712
I have a little bitcoin, but I need large amounts of cash on hand.

62274038
>>62273994
>DYOR
>he does not DYOR
Doesn't matter that much though since you can just keep scaling by putting gains into new assets rather than ever staking.

62274120
Let's buy more BTC

62274282
>>62273959
yes beause stocks represnt ownership in a company which is a real asset with actual cashflow unlike a digital ponzi based on a generic internet token with zero utility that anyone can make at any time

also im not pretending stocks are the future currency of the world that everyone is going to use like the retard dipshits such as yourself

62274315
>>62273655
>i dont care if it goes to 1million
the /biz/ way

62274703
>>62273994
You didn’t even look into how it works and already calling it a scam lol. At least research it first before getting emotional.

62274705
>>62271712
It needs to go down to $50, so I will buy them.

otherwise GTFO!

62274708
>>62271960
Most people only believe in BTC after the price already moves far beyond what they once thought was impossible.

62275419
>>62271712
I am always buying BTC.

62275451
I don't have tens of thousands of dollars to blow on just one type of investment. Sorry I noticed too late and was too financially illiterate to trust it over a decade ago.

62275467
>>62271712
>make gains elsewhere
>rotate into BTC
That's how I have to go about it being such a latecomer.

62275501
>>62271712
returns are too low at this point, and it can go to 0 at any time. why are you holding bags?

62276869
>>62272002
BTC doesn’t need to become the global reserve currency overnight to justify current valuations. You people still think adoption is binary for some reason.

62276880
>>62272017
CLARITY isn't going to happen, and the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve won't mean shit unless it is passed as a bill in Congress (which also won't happen)

62276888
Fear
I actually think crypto seems retarded. I also don't understand what makes Bitcoin any different than the 10,000 other shitcoins out there. Yeah Bitcoin was first, but that is the only think I can understand that makes it special compared to numerous other coins.

I feel extreme regret for not buying it. I just make myself feel better by telling myself I would have sold it when I hit my first 5x and I never would have been able to hodl this long.

I literally almost bought $100 of Bitcoin when it was like $5 each. I closed the window at the last moment because I thought it was stupid. It feels like throwing away a winning lottery card whenever I think about it.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 260 replies — 26.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 260 • Updated: 2026-05-26 12:47 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265896
>>62264378
>This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.
the stemfag market was goated 12 years ago, now you have to be the throte goat to find anything

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

62269694
>>62268709
It's pretty easy if you let her use a wand or clit sucking vibrator to give herself orgasms. The only tricky part is YOU cumming during one of her 5 or 8 orgasms.

62269751
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing
Why not? Are you a woman? It's usually only parasites annoyed by this.

62269890
>incel bait (((mysteriously))) ruins another anti waging thread
this reddit poster is obviously some low test gooner soiboi. In reality 1.9m in an index fund can get you around 150k a year which is more than enough for a non-incel life especially with a paid off house.

62269901
>>62259935
I'm 37 and once I hit a 2m net worth in the next couple of years I'm going to "retire" and go back into academia. It doesn't pay very well but at least the work is enjoyable and I get to spend time with other scientists instead of these bullshit artists in industry.

62269979
>>62259935
No women? The entire point of FIRE is to fuck wives while the husband works...did you even read the manifesto?

62270348
>>62259935
I'm 40 with only ~$400k net worth. No kids/wife/never been on a date. $25k annual spend.
Just get me a rope...

62270366
How dare you??
You are just a damn child.

62270374
>>62270348
I had $400k net worth when I was 40. I'm 44 now with $1.7 million net worth. A lot can change in a short amount of time.

62270394
>>62270374
>A lot can change in a short amount of time.
Bitcoin's growth is slower now and that's my only hope.
My salary is currently $100k and I don't see potential for any significant raises in my future.
At least I think I'll have the balls to exit the system at $1M and maybe find a part time job stocking shelves to keep me busy.

62270406
>>62270348
You're not missing anything. Society is fucked. I didn't start dating until I was ~33. One woman that I dated when I was 34 ruined me forever. Not financially - just mentally.

I'm worse off financially than you because of student loans but in a few more years, I'll be in better shape albeit still with like half of your net worth when I hit your age. You're in okay shape. You're doing much better than most of your peers. You can get the validation from a woman if you push for it but it won't help. I got validation from other women after the crazy one and I didn't feel a thing. Hopefully you have nieces and nephews for whom you can leave something behind. That's my plan even though I'll probably never even meet my nieces or nephews. So it goes.

62270414
>>62270406
Thanks, bro. I've got my music, I guess.
>nieces and nephews
Nah. I'm a single child raised by a single mother. Once she's gone, I'm all alone.

62270422
>>62270414
Sorry man. My nieces and nephews are the children of my cousins, not any siblings, and I don't have a relationship with my cousins. For all intents and purposes, I'll be pretty alone when my parents are gone and they're both up there in age.

Music is my thing too. I don't enjoy it as much anymore though. Nothing hits the same after that woman. You still have a lot of time to find someone especially since you have your shit together financially but just be careful. I don't want anyone to ever have to feel the way I do on account of a woman.

62270444
>>62270422
I hope things will work out for us in the end.
I'd definitely rather be in a band (something like Jaga Jazzist) than a relationship :) We'll see were things go.

62270945
>>62262891

OnlyFans is always hiring: no experience necessary, work from home at your own hours, no dress code required

62271089
>>62259968
so dont marry. i literally do not understand why you would if you have money and a house?
horny? hire a prostitute.
lonely? get friends, or see above.
existential crisis? realize its fake and gay, we all go into the ground eventually. dead and rotting in the ground.

62271100
>>62270366
i thought the chinese have a huge surplus of men
my mom got pregnant when she was 40 and had me. i only have a touch of 'tism

62271108
>>62268693
All the FIREs are also all bogles. They do not have the brains or cojones to try to get a +3%/year outperformance on the SPY (which I get easily in one of my bad but green months). They do not trust themselves that grand feat.

62271141
>>62263178
Does your "t" button not work?

62271142
>>62261104
Tom Brady is a very swell guy. Everyone should check out his book "The TB12 Method"

62271171
>>62269901
I'm 27, any tips and tricks?

62271254
>>62260803
cant I just do this with crypto and give my mommey the sneed phrase?

62274663
Never retire, goy.
